mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals accelerating economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving equity valuations higher within a one-week forward window.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0% over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based capacity expansion and durable demand, which jointly compresses risk premiums and lifts equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC (p > 0.10) when backtested against 20-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and consumer credit expansion indicates demand exhaustion, which precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite the divergence signal, invalidating the demand-exhaustion premise.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt 1-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points forces immediate repricing of duration risk, causing equity multiples to contract and driving negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances following a 25bp monthly rate hike over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the industrial production index reflects sustained manufacturing output growth, which directly supports corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes against 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth acceleration and unemployment stabilization confirm labor market resilience, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive equity valuations over a quarterly period.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant predictive Rank IC (p > 0.05) over any 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price levels combined with stagnant real GDP growth trigger stagflation expectations, which force central bank policy paralysis and compress equity valuations within a one-week window.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPI exceeds 220 alongside flat GDP growth, refuting the stagflation equity penalty.
